reasoning: According to recent polls from mid-to-late October 2025, Zohran Mamdani is holding a massive 15-to-20-point lead in the general NYC mayoral election, securing around 45-51% of the vote. Andrew Cuomo is in second with roughly 28-33%, while Curtis Sliwa is in third with 13-15%. Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) does NOT apply to NYC general elections, meaning the election relies on a standard plurality. Curtis Sliwa has repeatedly stated he will not drop out, ensuring the anti-Mamdani vote remains split between him and Cuomo. With early voting already underway as of October 25, the chance of Mamdani losing a ~15-point lead and dropping to second place behind Cuomo makes his odds of finishing exactly second exceptionally low, barring an unprecedented massive last-minute shock.
